one_time_description: BC Hydro Trust Funds Contribution of $3,841,681 (vs $155,175 prior year), likely a large one-time distribution from Victoria Foundation endowment trust
economic_notes: Nation received $567,706 in COVID-19 related funding ($458,303 from ISC, $109,403 from FNHA). Victoria Foundation Endowment Fund holds $13,500,000 initial capital contribution in perpetuity. Nation has investments in forestry, freight, outfitting, and energy partnerships.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to Nation not preparing an approved budget for FY2022, which is required under PSAS. Long-term debt includes RBC loans ($3,387,080) plus FNFA promissory note ($2,156,451) totaling $5,543,531. Five consolidated subsidiaries include: 0936269 B.C. Ltd., Kwadacha Settlement Negotiations Agreement, Kwadacha Community Store, Kwadacha Land Corp., and Kwadacha Community Restaurant. Six GBEs accounted for by modified equity: Kwadacha Master GP Ltd. (100%), Kwadacha Master LP (99.99%), Tse Keh Nay LLP (33.33%), Claw Mountain Outfitters LLP (50%), Three Feathers LP (33.33%), KNGV Freight Services JV (50%). Victoria Foundation endowment of $13,500,000 held in perpetuity and NOT recorded in financial statements. Tangible capital assets net book value not extractable from text provided. BC Hydro Trust Funds Contribution was exceptionally large this year ($3.8M vs $155K prior year).
